channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Sony State of Play June 2026 Showcase—Everything Announced
item.content:encoded:
From leaks to rumors, to competition with other gaming brands, this feels like one of the most crucial Sony State of Play Showcase events in a long time. To ensure you don’t miss a second of the action, you’re in the right place.

Sony needs to watch out, as XBOX is starting to become a serious gaming brand again, and Nintendo is killing it as always. Sony has been a bit quieter on the PlayStation front, where exclusives and big reasons to own a PS5 are concerned. Let’s also not forget that XBOX is lowering Game Pass prices and listening to its audience again, whereas PlayStation hardware is getting more expensive—as is PlayStation Plus’ price.

Sony needs a big event to kickstart Summer Game Fest. Expect some major announcements.

All Sony State of Play June 2026 Showcase Announcements

If this isn’t your first rodeo with us, you know the score by now: We will keep updating this hub with every new announcement, game reveal, and new trailer as the show goes on.

This is a live, minute-by-minute hub.

  • More Insider Gaming: Official Summer Game Fest 2026 Event Calendar

If you can’t follow Sony’s State of Play event for whatever reason, then this is the perfect page to refresh for quickfire updates on what’s shown during the PlayStation Showcase.

What’s Expected From The June 2026 Sony State of Play?

Wolverine, a new God of War game, Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet, and a rebranded Fairgame$ are just some of the exciting games that could be shown.

The next look at PlayStation’s Wolverine was confirmed by Sony for State of Play. It’s the only certain appearance we know of beforehand, making everything else speculation and rumors.

However, if you’re interested, we have a separate, in-depth ‘all confirmed & rumored games for Sony State Of Play 2026‘ hub, where we’ve gathered up every single detail, rumor, leak, and more ahead of the June 2026 SOP.

It’s expected to be a 60-90-minute show, and it might be time to break the popcorn out, if expectations are anything to go by.

Chat with the IG community live throughout the State of Play presentation through our free Insider Gaming Discord. Also, pop down after the show to say what you liked and disliked about the whole showcase! Did it deliver?

There has been a lot of news recently, so catch up on some recent, hot gaming content:

  • Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis Releases In 2027
  • Hell Let Loose Vietnam Delayed By Team17
  • Unexpected New Yooka-Laylee Game Discovered Online
  • Maverick Games Officially Reveals CLUTCH After Weeks-Long Teaser

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

item.title: Esports Nations Cup Trackmania Invites Revealed
item.content:encoded:
The Esports Foundation has revealed the latest set of representatives and invitees for this year’s Esports Nations Cup, this time in Trackmania.

The first iteration of the nations-based esports tournament will take place between November 2 and 29.

We’ve already seen a similar announce for another car-based game in the Rocket League Esports Nations Cup rosters.

Now, it has been confirmed which 16 countries have been directly invited to compete in the $250,000 Trackmania event.

The Trackmania tournament at ENC will begin on November 19 and will conclude with the playoff final on November 22.

All 32 players will first compete in a group stage, featuring four groups of eight players.

Then, 16 players (the top four of each group) will compete in a single-elimination playoff bracket. During the Playoffs, once players hit the points threshold, they must then secure a round win to claim the match.

Esports Nations Cup 2026 Invited Nations and Players

Trackmania at the Esports Nations Cup will feature 32 individuals representing their countries.

However, 16 players and nations have already received direct invites to the event.

The Esports Foundation used Ubisfoft’s Trackmania Elite Cup Rankings to determine the highest-ranked players from each nation.

The 16 invited players and nations/territories are:

  • France – Alexandre ‘Binkss’ Touche
  • Germany – Nico ‘GranaDy’ Gyarmati
  • Australia – Patrick ‘Mudda’ Radisich
  • Czechia – Filip ‘eLconn21’ Šprungl
  • Canada – Carl-Antoni ‘Carl Jr.’ Cloutier
  • Belgium– Alister ‘Soulja’ Adutwum
  • Poland – Hubert ‘mime’ Dziedzic 
  • Great Britain – Thomas ‘Pac’ Cole
  • Switzerland – Sébastien ‘Affi’ Affolter
  • Netherlands – Mees ‘Stufts’ Jenema
  • Hungary – Péter ‘Pusztitopako’ Varga
  • Sweden – ‘Molle’
  • Slovenia– ‘Jann’
  • Slovakia – Marek ‘tween’ Pacher
  • Russia – Vsevolod ‘NiTech’ Kozlov
  • Türkiye – Cem ‘Cemko’ Akyürek

Spare a thought for Germany’s Dennis ‘Massa’ Lotze, who is third in the rankings one place behind GranaDy, just missed out.

Also, the French trio of Maxence ‘Nayko’ Cloud, Gwendal ‘Gwen’ Duparc, and Youen ‘Otaaaq’ Egler are all in the Trackmania top 10 but couldn’t compete with first-place Binkss.

Also Croatia and the United States were the closest nations to miss out direct qualification with Robert ‘Razii’ Čupković and Flimsy ranking 40 and 45 respectively, just behind Türkiye’s 36-rated Cemko.

However, online qualifiers set to take place June 19-21 will fill the remaining 16 spots in the competition, with each nation allowed up to two players in total at the ENC.

Who do you expect to win the Trackmania ENC? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Meanwhile, the Esports Foundation has also announced how Wildcard invites will work in the Esports Nations Cup.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Tomb Raider: Legacy Of Atlantis Deluxe Edition Details Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis is launching in February 2027, and we have details on what players will get should they decide to purchase the Deluxe Edition of the game.

The details come from an Amazon store listing for the game that has since been taken down.

Retailing for $69.99, the Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis Deluxe Edition comes with a copy of the base game. But it also comes with some extras.

If you pick up the Deluxe version of the game ahead of launch, you’ll get 48-hour early access starting on February 8. In addition, players will get an early unlock of the “Parisian Outfit” as well as the post-launch DLC story pack at no extra cost.

Those who pre-order any edition of the game will also get an early unlock of the “Survivor Outfit”.

Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis is scheduled to release on February 10, 2027, for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC.

What Is Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis?

Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis is a complete remake of the original Tomb Raider from 1996. In development at Crystal Dynamics, the game is being published by Amazon Game Studios and is one of two Tomb Raider titles in development.

The game’s description is as follows:

“As Lara Croft, harness your wits and athleticism to explore exotic locations lost to time—from the jungles of Peru to the ancient ruins of Greece, the deserts of Egypt, and a mysterious Mediterranean island shrouded in myth. Traverse treacherous landscapes, solve deadly contraptions, and face lethal predators as you hunt for the scattered pieces of the Scion, an artifact of immeasurable power.”

In other news, check out everything that was announced as part of the inaugural Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, including 15 world premieres. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis Releases In 2027
item.content:encoded:
A reimagined take on Lara Croft’s first adventure will be in the hands of players as of early 2027.

A Twitter advertisement from Wario 64 states that “Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis” is scheduled for release on February 10, 2027. The post included a link that appeared to be an Amazon page; however, it currently returns a “page not found” error. The game is expected to be priced at $59.99 for the standard version on Steam, with the deluxe edition available for pre-order at $69.99. The pre-order bonus content features a new outfit for Lara; there’s also promised post-launch DLC story content.

More on Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis

The game was announced during the 2025 Game Awards to celebrate the 30th anniversary of the original release of Tomb Raider on the Sony PlayStation and Sega Saturn. Additionally, a new Lara Croft adventure, Tomb Raider: Catalyst, was announced at the same time. That game currently doesn’t have any prospective release dates.

“2026 marks the 30th anniversary of the Tomb Raider series. As such, it is the perfect moment to honor Lara Croft’s beginning and introduce her debut adventure to a whole new audience. Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis, co-developed with our talented friends at Flying Wild Hog,  is a true reimagining of the original game. We’ve thoughtfully refreshed the experience in exciting ways. Also built from the ground up in UE5, it features a gorgeous re-envisioning of iconic environments, immersive sound design, modern gameplay mechanics, and new surprises, all while honoring the spirit of the classic,” the team behind the game said when it was first announced.

For more Tomb Raider news, check out casting news tying the forthcoming TV series based around the legendary game franchise to the original game.

Are you excited for the forthcoming Legacy of Atlantis game? Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: COMPUTEX 2026: Corsair Announces Lineup Of New Products
item.content:encoded:
COMPUTEX 2026 is taking place in Taiwan this week, and companies from around the world are announcing their lineups of new consumer products heading to market. One company that’s expanding its peripheral offerings for players is Corsair, announcing four new products at the event.

First, two new gaming headsets were revealed: The Corsair HS35 v3 and v3 wireless.

These headsets are entry-level devices, featuring custom-tuned 50mm neodymium drivers. The wired version, retailing for $49.99, features a 3.5mm jack that can connect to PC, consoles, and mobile devices. If you want the wireless version at $79.99, it can connect via 2.4Ghz adapter or Bluetooth.

“A redesigned adjustable floating headband and breathable soft fabric earcups keep the headset comfortable through long play sessions,” Corsair says.

The wireless headset comes in at 250g and features a detachable omnidirectional mic and up to 30 hours of battery life. For the wired, the microphone isn’t detachable, but the headset is slightly lighter at 230g.

Aside from headsets, a new keyboard and mouse were announced.

The Corsair Clipper Pro Mini 60 Hall Effect Gaming Keyboard—that’s a mouthful, for sure—adds hall effect into a 60% layout. Retailing for $99.99, it allows up to four different actions to a single key, tap lock that turns hold actions into toggles, and a smart tap feature that lets users assign separate actions to quick taps.

Also included are new MGX Hyperdrive Core magnetic switches.

“As the first CORSAIR keyboard with these new switches, players will enjoy adjustable actuation from 0.2mm to 3.8mm with consistent performance across 100 million keystrokes,” Corsair says. “A square stem structure reduces wobble for crisper switch feel and more precise actuation.”

Lastly, the Nightsword v2 Wireless Stream Deck mouse is the first mouse to feature a dedicated Stream Deck launch button.

“One press of the Stream Deck Launch Button summons an Elgato Virtual Stream Deck overlay directly on-screen, built around whatever game or genre you are playing,” Corsair said. “Set up profiles for easy ability chains, inventory shortcuts, media controls, and more. Virtual Stream Decks can be triggered from 8 programmable buttons on the NIGHTSWORD v2 WIRELESS SD, and you can natively map functions to the mouse with the Stream Deck app.”

Every product is available starting today, June 2, and can be purchased from Corsair or a partner retailer.

What do you think of the products Corsair unveiled? Do any stand out to you? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, or join the discussion in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, check out the new ASUS ROG XBOX Ally X20 that features an OLED display. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw
